mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.4% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 persons, signaling weakening labor demand that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 144,952 thousand requires month-over-month comparison
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth < 100,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,000 thousand units, indicating synchronized demand contraction across consumer and residential sectors that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate < 2% annualized AND HOUST < 1,000 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 465,914 million and HOUST at 1,047 thousand units near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of co-triggered instances (both conditions met), or Spearman rank correlation between interaction signal and forward returns < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2%, indicating supply-side contraction coupled with labor slack that signals stagflationary pressure and negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 104.226 and UNRATE at 4.9% require monitoring for trigger activation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal exhibits Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation, supporting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 241.002 and FEDFUNDS at 0.4% suggest negative real rates
regime_hint: inflationary environment with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, indicating demand weakness that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 16,702.1 billion (chained 2017 dollars) requires quarterly growth calculation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines below 4.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 150,000 persons monthly, indicating tight labor market conditions that support consumer income growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% AND PAYEMS monthly growth > 150,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.9% and PAYEMS at 144,952 thousand near trigger thresholds
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong employment growth
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.05 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2.0% annualized rate, indicating expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength that supports corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 104.226 (index 2017=100) requires month-over-month acceleration assessment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during periods of INDPRO growth > 2.0% annualized are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
